summary: Closed, unmerged gmregclear decomp PR that matched two functions, fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C, updated their header prototypes, and exposed a s16 field at lbl_80472D28_t offset 0x10A. The PR body claimed clean ninja and 100.0 fuzzy match for all functions. A later comment says the work was consolidated into other PRs, so this slice is useful mainly as evidence for matching tactics and type/layout decisions rather than as a merged change.

key_files.reason: Replaced nonmatching placeholders for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C and refined lbl_80472D28_t layout.
Changed fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures.

what_changed: Added a C implementation for fn_8017DE54 with signature s32 fn_8017DE54(u8 arg0, u8* arg1). The logic checks bit flags in arg0: bit 0x20 returns 0, bit 0x10 counts entries among arg1[0..2] that are not 0x21 and returns 3 - count, bit 0x2 returns 2, otherwise returns 0.

Added a C implementation for fn_8017F14C with signature s32 fn_8017F14C(void* arg0). It checks gm_8016AE38()->x24C8.x4_7, reads an s32 from arg0 + 0x98, clamps values above 9999, multiplies by lbl_80472D28.x10A, and otherwise returns 0.
Refined struct lbl_80472D28_t by splitting padding at offset 0x108 to expose a s16 field named x10A at offset 0x10A before x116.
Updated gmregclear.h prototypes for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C from unknown return/parameter macros to concrete s32 return types and explicit parameters.
The PR was closed without merge; the author comment states it was consolidated into other PRs.
smart_moves: Exposed the known global data field at offset 0x10A as x10A instead of leaving all of the area as anonymous padding, which made fn_8017F14C cleaner while preserving offset-based naming.
Kept the arg0 + 0x98 access in fn_8017F14C as explicit pointer math because the parameter remained void* and no known decompiled callers established a safe struct type.
Documented why a tempting struct-field replacement was not made: offset 0x98 fell inside unnamed padding in the closest candidate struct, so inventing a field would be overconfident.
Used narrow/offset-driven prototypes and field names rather than semantic names not supported by the available evidence.
Updated the header prototypes in the same PR as the function bodies, making the decompilation reusable by later code.
decomp_lessons: When a pointer argument has unknown concrete type and no known callers, explicit u8* pointer arithmetic can be the safest matching representation.
Do not promote an offset inside another struct's unnamed padding to a named field unless the actual struct ownership is known.
For global/static data layouts, splitting padding to expose a single confirmed field is acceptable when the offset and type are needed for a matching function.
Keep placeholder-style names such as fn_8017F14C and x10A when behavior or semantics are not yet established.
Closed/unmerged PRs can still preserve useful matching patterns, but final symbol status should be checked in the later consolidated PRs.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions stayed in address-based form, e.g. fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C.
Anonymous data structs used label-derived names such as lbl_80472D28_t and global lbl_80472D28.
Unidentified struct members used offset names such as x10A, with padding names such as pad_108 and pad_10C.
Header comments retained address markers like /* 17DE54 */ and /* 17F14C */ beside prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved byte/word access patterns with u8 arguments and u8* indexing in fn_8017DE54.
Used explicit casts to s32 around byte comparisons against 0x21, likely to control generated comparison/codegen shape.
Introduced a temporary pointer p = &arg1[1] in fn_8017DE54, matching the access sequence for arg1[1] and arg1[2].
Implemented fn_8017F14C's unknown-struct access as *(s32*) ((u8*) arg0 + 0x98), keeping exact byte offset arithmetic.
Clamped the loaded value to 9999 before multiplying by the s16 global field x10A.
The PR body reports ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for the matched functions.

review_feedback.text: The author noted pointer math would be fixed soon, then followed up that the pointer math in fn_8017F14C could not be replaced with struct field access because arg0 is void*, has no known decompiled callers, and offset 0x98 lies in unnamed padding in the closest candidate struct.
A later comment says the PR was consolidated into other PRs.
No review comments or formal reviews were present in this slice.

follow_up_queries: Find the later consolidated PRs that absorbed fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C.
Check current master for the final signatures and implementations of fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C.
Search for decompiled callers of fn_8017F14C to identify the real type of its void* arg0 and the field at offset 0x98.
Verify whether lbl_80472D28_t.x10A kept the same type and offset after consolidation.
